title: Improve exception-safety meta-test robustness in test_stages.py
analysis:
The test `test_patch_stage_restores_on_exception` at line 632 of test_stages.py has two robustness gaps: (1) it uses `try/except RuntimeError: pass` which never verifies the exception actually propagated — if `_patch_stage` swallowed the exception, the test would still pass (false green), and (2) if `_patch_stage.__exit__` raises a different exception type during teardown, the `except RuntimeError` block won't catch it and postcondition assertions are skipped entirely.

The fix is straightforward: replace the `try/except` with `pytest.raises(RuntimeError, match='boom')` as the outer context manager, and keep postconditions after the `with` block. This pattern is already used extensively in the same file (lines 459, 473, 487, 876, 890, 909, etc.) so it aligns perfectly with existing conventions.

With `pytest.raises`, the test gains two properties: (a) it explicitly asserts the exception propagated (failing if it was swallowed), and (b) postconditions after the `with` block always execute because `pytest.raises` catches the expected exception — if an unexpected exception occurs, pytest reports it as a test error, which is the correct behavior.

This is a test-only change — no production code is modified. The `_patch_stage` helper (line 426) uses `unittest.mock.patch` context managers which properly restore on exceptions, so the improved test should pass immediately.

steps.description: Rewrite test_patch_stage_restores_on_exception to use pytest.raises(RuntimeError, match='boom') instead of try/except, and move postcondition assertions outside the pytest.raises block. This makes the test fail if the exception is swallowed (gap 1) and ensures postconditions always run after pytest.raises succeeds (gap 2). The rewritten test should pass immediately since _patch_stage correctly propagates exceptions.

Run the test suite for test_stages.py to verify the improved test passes. Confirm no regressions in adjacent _patch_stage tests (test_patch_stage_patches_assemble_payload_and_run_stage, test_patch_stage_accepts_cli_side_effect).

design_decisions.decision: Use pytest.raises(RuntimeError, match='boom') instead of try/except + else: pytest.fail()
design_decisions.rationale: The task description offers two options. pytest.raises is preferred because: (a) it's already the dominant pattern in this file (9 other uses), (b) it's more concise, (c) match='boom' adds message verification for free, and (d) postconditions naturally fall outside the with block, always executing after a successful catch.

design_decisions.decision: Keep postconditions outside the with block rather than in a finally block
design_decisions.rationale: With pytest.raises as the outer context manager, postconditions after the with block always run when the expected exception is caught. If an unexpected exception occurs, pytest reports a test error — which is the correct diagnostic behavior. A finally block would mask the unexpected exception with postcondition assertion failures.

reuse.how: The same pattern is already used 9 times in this file. The fix adopts the identical idiom: `with pytest.raises(RuntimeError, match='boom'):` wrapping the inner `with self._patch_stage(stage):` block.
